title: Diving Reflex
script_summary: Exploring the diving reflex and its fascinating science without exaggeration.

segments.voiceover: Ever wondered why your body holds its breath underwater?
It's called the diving reflex, a survival mechanism in many animals, including humans.
When submerged, your heart rate slows, and blood flow redirects to vital organs.
Studies show this reflex is stronger in cold water and essential for survival.
But here's the catch—our understanding of it is still evolving.
Curious about more science insights? Follow for more!
